title: Binary Search: Missing Check for Single Element Array

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array arr and a target value target, return the first index i that target is present in arr starting from index i. If target is not found in arr, return -1. If the array contains only one element and it is equal to target, return 0.
statement.notes: The array will contain at least one element, and each element is at most 104.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

hints.text: First hint text: Be careful when handling edge cases such as a single-element array.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int search(int A[], int n, int target) { // TODO: implement}

starterCode.python: class Solution: def search(self, A: list[int], n: int, target: int) -> int: pass
starterCode.javascript: function solve(A, n, target) { // TODO: implement

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int search(int A[], int n, int target) { if (n == 0) return 0; int left = 0, right = n - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (A[mid] == target) return mid; else if (A[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return -1; }
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution {public int search(int[] A, int n, int target) { if (n == 0) return 0; int left = 0, right = n - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (A[mid] == target) return mid; else if (A[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return -1; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def search(self, A: list[int], n: int, target: int) -> int: if n == 0: return 0; left, right = 0, n - 1; while left <= right: mid = left + (right - left) // 2; if A[mid] == target: return mid; elif A[mid] < target: left = mid + 1; else: right = mid - 1; return -1;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(A, n, target) { if (n == 0) return 0; var left = 0, right = n - 1; while (left <= right) { var mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (A[mid] == target) return mid; else if (A[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return -1;

functionSignature.cpp: int search(int A[], int n, int target);
functionSignature.java: int search(int[] A, int n, int target);
functionSignature.python: int search(self, A: list[int], n: int, target: int) -> int;

solutions.python: class Solution: def search(self, A: list[int], n: int, target: int) -> int: if n == 1: return 0; if A[0] == target: return 0; if n == 0: return -1; left, right = 0, n - 1; while left <= right: mid = left + (right - left) // 2; if A[mid] == target: return mid; elif A[mid] < target: left = mid + 1; else: right = mid - 1; return -1; def main(): A = [int(x) for x in input().split(' ')] n = int(input()) target = int(input()) result = Solution().search(A, n, target) print(result) main()
solutions.cpp: class Solution {public: int search(int A[], int n, int target) { if (n == 1) return 0; if (A[0] == target) return 0; if (n == 0) return -1; int left = 0, right = n - 1; while (left <= right) { int mid = left + (right - left) / 2; if (A[mid] == target) return mid; else if (A[mid] < target) left = mid + 1; else right = mid - 1; } return -1; } }; int main() { int A[10000]; int n, target; std::cin >> n; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) std::cin >> A[i]; std::cin >> target; std::cout << Solution().search(A, n, target) << std::endl; return 0;}

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
